- `nanos` (Number) Signed fractions of a second at nanosecond resolution of the span of time. Durations less than one second are represented with a 0 `seconds` field and a positive or negative `nanos` field. For durations of one second or more, a non-zero value for the `nanos` field must be of the same sign as the `seconds` field. Must be from -999,999,999 to +999,999,999 inclusive.
- `seconds` (Number) Signed seconds of the span of time. Must be from -315,576,000,000 to +315,576,000,000 inclusive. Note: these bounds are computed from: 60 sec/min * 60 min/hr * 24 hr/day * 365.25 days/year * 10000 years







<a id="nestedatt--defaults--waf_settings"></a>
### Nested Schema for `defaults.waf_settings`

Required:

- `rules` (List of String) Rules to be leveraged by WAF. The parser evaluates the list of rules from the top to the bottom.


<a id="nestedatt--defaults--wasm_extensions"></a>
### Nested Schema for `defaults.wasm_extensions`

Optional:

- `config` (String) Configuration parameters sent to the WASM plugin execution. This configuration will overwrite the one specified globally in the extension. This config will be passed as-is to the extension. It is up to the extension to deserialize the config and use it.
- `match` (Attributes List) Specifies the criteria to determine which traffic is passed to WasmExtension. If a traffic satisfies any of TrafficSelectors, the traffic passes to the WasmExtension. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--defaults--wasm_extensions--match))

<a id="nestedatt--defaults--wasm_extensions--match"></a>
### Nested Schema for `defaults.wasm_extensions.match`

Optional:

- `mode` (String) Criteria for selecting traffic by their direction. Note that CLIENT and SERVER are analogous to OUTBOUND and INBOUND, respectively. For the gateway, the field should be CLIENT or CLIENT_AND_SERVER. If not specified, the default value is CLIENT_AND_SERVER.
- `ports` (Attributes List) Criteria for selecting traffic by their destination port. More specifically, for the outbound traffic, the destination port would be the port of the target service. On the other hand, for the inbound traffic, the destination port is the port bound by the server process in the same Pod. If one of the given `ports` is matched, this condition is evaluated to true. If not specified, this condition is evaluated to true for any port. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--defaults--wasm_extensions--match--ports))

<a id="nestedatt--defaults--wasm_extensions--match--ports"></a>
### Nested Schema for `defaults.wasm_extensions.match.ports`

Required:

- `number` (Number) Port number





<a id="nestedatt--mandates"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandates`

Optional:

- `authentication_settings` (Attributes) Authentication settings is used to set workload-to-workload traffic and end-user/origin authentication configuration. This will be moved under a `security` section in the future. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--authentication_settings))
- `traffic` (Attributes) Traffic settings for proxy workloads. Proxy workloads without a specific traffic group will inherit these settings. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--traffic))
- `traffic_settings` (Attributes) Traffic settings for proxy workloads. Proxy workloads without a specific traffic group will inherit these settings. Deprecated in favor of the traffic field, which has no deprecated inner fields. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--traffic_settings))
- `unset_fields` (List of String) Unset fields specify fields that must not have any value. In Mandates, fields in this list must remain unset, even if subsequent evaluated Profile Mandates define them. In Defaults, fields in this list are removed if defined in previously evaluated Profile Defaults. Items in this list are dot-separated paths to the fields, relative to the root of ProfileConfig. Field names are in camelCase, as in JSON/YAML. Paths that navigate lists or maps unset the sub-path for all elements. For example: - "traffic.outbound.reachability.hosts" - "traffic.inbound.resilience.meshTimeout.maxConnectionDuration"
- `waf_settings` (Attributes) WAF settings is used to set firewall rules. The WAF rules configured here only apply to workloads, not gateways. This will be moved under a `security` section in the future. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--waf_settings))
- `wasm_extensions` (Attributes List) Wasm Extensions specifies all the WasmExtensions assigned to this profile with the specific configuration for each extension. The WASM extensions configured here only apply to workloads, not gateways. This will be moved under a `security` section in the future. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--wasm_extensions))

<a id="nestedatt--mandates--authentication_settings"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandates.authentication_settings`

Optional:

- `http` (Attributes) HTTP request authentication is used to configure authentication of origin/end-user credentials like JSON Web Token (JWT). It is highly recommended to set traffic authentication mode to REQUIRED so that it is transported only over mutual TLS (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--authentication_settings--http))
- `traffic_mode` (String) Traffic authentication mode is used to specify if mTLS or plaintext traffic is accepted

<a id="nestedatt--mandates--authentication_settings--http"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandates.authentication_settings.http`

Optional:

- `jwt` (Attributes) Authenticate an HTTP request from a JWT Token attached to it. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--authentication_settings--http--jwt))
- `oidc` (Attributes) Authenticate an HTTP request using an external OIDC provider (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--authentication_settings--http--oidc))
- `require_jwt` (Attributes) If set to true, JWT authentication is mandatory and enforced with strict validation. Requests without a valid JWT token will be rejected with a 401 (Unauthorized) error. This field ONLY applies when JWT authentication is configured (via 'jwt' or 'rules'). It is IGNORED when OIDC is configured. Behavior when JWT is configured: - When false (default): JWT tokens are optional, but if provided, they must be valid.  Requests without tokens are allowed to pass through. - When true: All requests must have a valid JWT token from one of the configured providers, otherwise they receive a 401 response.   Note that to use this feature xcp.enable_jwt_authentication_require_jwt_token_setting needs to be set to true on controlplane CR. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--authentication_settings--http--require_jwt))
- `rules` (Attributes) List of rules how to authenticate an HTTP request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--authentication_settings--http--rules))

<a id="nestedatt--mandates--authentication_settings--http--jwt"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandates.authentication_settings.http.jwt`

Required:

- `issuer` (String) Identifies the issuer that issued the JWT. See [issuer](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7519#section-4.1.1) A JWT with different `iss` claim will be rejected. Example: https://foobar.auth0.com Example: 1234567-compute@developer.gserviceaccount.com

Optional:

- `audiences` (List of String) The list of JWT [audiences](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7519#section-4.1.3). that are allowed to access. A JWT containing any of these audiences will be accepted. The service name will be accepted if audiences is empty.
- `from_cookies` (List of String) List of cookie names from which JWT is expected. For example, if config is:  fromCookies:  - auth-token Then JWT will be extracted from auth-token cookie in the request. Note: Requests with multiple tokens (at different locations) are not supported. The behaviour of authorization policies when there is more than one user identity is undefined
- `from_headers` (Attributes List) This field specifies the locations to extract JWT token. If no explicit location is specified the following default locations are tried in order:     1) The Authorization header using the Bearer schema,        e.g. Authorization: Bearer <token>. (see        [Authorization Request Header        Field](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6750#section-2.1))     2) The `access_token` query parameter (see     [URI Query Parameter](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6750#section-2.3)) List of header locations from which JWT is expected. For example, below is the location spec if JWT is expected to be found in `x-jwt-assertion` header, and have `Bearer ` prefix: ```yaml   fromHeaders:   - name: x-jwt-assertion     prefix: "Bearer " ``` Note: Multiple tokens present on the same request are not supported.  The behaviour of authorization policies when there is more than one user identity is undefined (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--authentication_settings--http--jwt--from_headers))
- `jwks` (String) JSON Web Key Set of public keys to validate signature of the JWT. See https://auth0.com/docs/jwks. Note: Only one of jwks_uri and jwks should be used. jwks_uri will be ignored if it does.
- `jwks_uri` (String) URL of the provider's public key set to validate signature of the JWT. See [OpenID Discovery](https://openid.net/specs/openid-connect-discovery-1_0.html#ProviderMetadata). Optional if the key set document can either (a) be retrieved from [OpenID Discovery](https://openid.net/specs/openid-connect-discovery-1_0.html) of the issuer or (b) inferred from the email domain of the issuer (e.g. a Google service account). Example: `https://www.googleapis.com/oauth2/v1/certs` Note: Only one of jwks_uri and jwks should be used. jwks_uri will be ignored if it does.
- `output_claim_to_headers` (Attributes List) This field specifies a list of operations to copy the claim to HTTP headers on a successfully verified token. This differs from the `output_payload_to_header` by allowing outputting individual claims instead of the whole payload. Only claims of type string, boolean, and integer are supported. Array type claims are not supported at this time. The header specified in each operation in the list must be unique. Nested claims of type string/int/bool is supported as well. ```   outputClaimToHeaders:   - header: x-my-company-jwt-group     claim: my-group   - header: x-test-environment-flag     claim: test-flag   - header: x-jwt-claim-group     claim: nested.key.group ``` [Experimental] This feature is a experimental feature. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--authentication_settings--http--jwt--output_claim_to_headers))
- `output_payload_to_header` (String) This field specifies the header name to output a successfully verified JWT payload to the backend. The forwarded data is `base64_encoded(jwt_payload_in_JSON)`. If it is not specified, the payload will not be emitted.

<a id="nestedatt--mandates--authentication_settings--http--jwt--from_headers"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandates.authentication_settings.http.jwt.from_headers`

Required:

- `name` (String) The HTTP header name.

Optional:

- `prefix` (String) The prefix that should be stripped before decoding the token. For example, for `Authorization: Bearer <token>`, prefix=`Bearer ` with a space at the end. If the header doesn't have this exact prefix, it is considered invalid.


<a id="nestedatt--mandates--authentication_settings--http--jwt--output_claim_to_headers"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandates.authentication_settings.http.jwt.output_claim_to_headers`

Required:

- `claim` (String) The name of the claim to be copied from. Only claim of type string/int/bool is supported. The header will not be there if the claim does not exist or the type of the claim is not supported.
- `header` (String) The name of the header to be created. The header will be overridden if it already exists in the request.



<a id="nestedatt--mandates--authentication_settings--http--oidc"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandates.authentication_settings.http.oidc`

Required:

- `client_id` (String) The client_id to be used in the authorize calls. This value will be URL encoded when sent to the OAuth server.
- `client_token_secret` (String) The name of the Kubernetes secret containing the client secret. Kubernetes generic opaque secret should contain `istio_generic_secret` key with base64 encoded client_secret as value.  For example --- apiVersion: v1 metadata:   name: bar   namespace: foo data:   istio_generic_secret: e2Jhc2U2NF9lbmNvZGVkX3Rva2VuX3NlY3JldH0= kind: Secret type: Opaque The secret must be present in the same namespace as the gateway or sidecar deployment for which the configuration is being applied for. The (gateway/ sidecar) deployment must also have RBAC permissions to view secrets in the current namespace. For gateways this is already configured, while for sidecar the permission should be added if not already present. The secret token stored will be URL encoded when sent to the OAuth server.
- `provider` (Attributes) The OIDC Provider configuration. If only `issuer` is provided, the config will be treated as `dynamic` and will be fetched from the well known endpoint. If more fields are configured, the configuration will be treated as `static` and thus should be complete. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--authentication_settings--http--oidc--provider))
- `redirect_uri` (String) The redirect URI passed to the authorization endpoint It can also be formulated from request parameters For example: %REQ(x-forwarded-proto)%://%REQ(:authority)%/callback This URI should not contain any query parameters.

Optional:

- `auth_scopes` (List of String) Optional list of OAuth scopes to be claimed in the authorization request. If not specified, defaults to `user` scope. OAuth RFC https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6749#section-3.3
- `auth_type` (String) Defines how client_id and client_secret are sent in OAuth client to OAuth server requests. RFC https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc6749#section-2.3.1
- `grant_type` (String) Configure the [authorization grant type to be used](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc6749#section-1.3)
- `redirect_path_matcher` (String) Matching criteria used to determine whether a path appears to be the result of a redirect from the authorization server. The query and fragment string (if present) are removed in the URL path portion. For example, the path `/data` will match URI header `/data#fragment?param=value`. If not provided, default is derived from redirect_uri path Only exact match is configurable
- `signout_path` (String) The path to sign a user out, clearing their credential cookies. If not provided, default is `/signout` Only exact match is configurable
- `use_refresh_token` (Attributes) Enable automatic access token refresh using associated refresh token [(see RFC 6749 section 6)](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc6749#section-6) provided that the OAuth server supports that. If not set defaults to `true`. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--authentication_settings--http--oidc--use_refresh_token))

<a id="nestedatt--mandates--authentication_settings--http--oidc--provider"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandates.authentication_settings.http.oidc.provider`

Required:

- `issuer` (String) The OIDC Provider's [issuer identifier](https://openid.net/specs/openid-connect-discovery-1_0.html#IssuerDiscovery).

Optional:

- `authorization_endpoint` (String) The OIDC Provider's [authorization endpoint](https://openid.net/specs/openid-connect-core-1_0.html#AuthorizationEndpoint). If not provided, it will be discovered from the provider's [Well-Known Configuration Endpoint](https://openid.net/specs/openid-connect-discovery-1_0.html#ProviderConfigurationResponse).
- `jwks` (String) JSON string with the OIDC provider's JSON Web Key Sets. In general the URI for the Key Set is the preferred method for configuring JWKS. This setting is provided in case the provider doesn't publish JWKS via a public URI.
- `jwks_uri` (String) URI for the OIDC provider's JSON Web Key Sets. This can be found in the OIDC provider's configuration response. The JWKS are used for token verification.
- `tls` (Attributes) The TLS settings used by the clients to connect with the OIDC provider. If only the OIDC provider Issuer is specified, TSB will fetch the well-known configuration using the specified TLS settings. Proxies will be configured to verify the TLS certificates provided by the OIDC provider using the CA cert provided in TLS settings. If TLS settings are not defined, TSB uses the system default CA certificates to fetch the well-known configuration and proxies will not validate the TLS certificates provided by the OIDC provider. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--authentication_settings--http--oidc--provider--tls))
- `token_endpoint` (String) The OIDC Provider's [token endpoint](https://openid.net/specs/openid-connect-core-1_0.html#TokenEndpoint). If not provided, it will be discovered from the provider's [Well-Known Configuration Endpoint](https://openid.net/specs/openid-connect-discovery-1_0.html#ProviderConfigurationResponse).

<a id="nestedatt--mandates--authentication_settings--http--oidc--provider--tls"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandates.authentication_settings.http.oidc.provider.tls`

Optional:

- `files` (Attributes) TLS key source from files. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--authentication_settings--http--oidc--provider--tls--files))
- `mode` (String) Set this to DISABLED to disable TLS (not recommended from the security perspective), SIMPLE for one-way TLS and MUTUAL for mutual TLS (where client is required to present its certificate as well)
- `secret_name` (String) TLS key source from a Kubernetes Secret. This is applicable for gateway workloads.
- `subject_alt_names` (List of String) Subject alternative names is the list of names that are accepted as service name as part of TLS handshake

<a id="nestedatt--mandates--authentication_settings--http--oidc--provider--tls--files"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandates.authentication_settings.http.oidc.provider.tls.files`

Optional:

- `ca_certificates` (String) File containing CA certificates to verify the certificates presented by the server. This is mandatory for both simple and mutual TLS. Here are some common paths for the system CA bundle on Linux and can be specified here if the server certificate is signed by a well known authority, already part of the system CA bundle on the host -    /etc/ssl/certs/ca-certificates.crt (Debian/Ubuntu/Gentoo etc.)   /etc/pki/ca-trust/extracted/pem/tls-ca-bundle.pem (CentOS/RHEL 7)   /etc/pki/tls/certs/ca-bundle.crt (Fedora/RHEL 6)
- `client_certificate` (String) Certificate file to authenticate the client. This is mandatory for mutual TLS and must not be specified for simple (one-way) TLS
- `private_key` (String) Private key file associated with the client certificate. This is mandatory for mutual TLS and must not be specified for simple TLS




<a id="nestedatt--mandates--authentication_settings--http--oidc--use_refresh_token"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandates.authentication_settings.http.oidc.use_refresh_token`

Optional:

- `value` (Boolean) The bool value.



<a id="nestedatt--mandates--authentication_settings--http--require_jwt"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandates.authentication_settings.http.require_jwt`

Optional:

- `value` (Boolean) The bool value.


<a id="nestedatt--mandates--authentication_settings--http--rules"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandates.authentication_settings.http.rules`

Optional:

- `jwt` (Attributes List) List of rules how to authenticate an HTTP request from a JWT Token attached to it. A JWT Token, if present in the HTTP request, must satisfy one of the rules defined here. The order in which rules are being checked at runtime might differ from the order in which they are defined here. If the JWT Token doesn't satisfy any of the rules, the request will be rejected. If the JWT Token does satisfy one of the rules, the identity of the request will be extracted from the JWT Token. Notice that an HTTP request without a JWT Token attached to it will NOT be rejected based on the rules defined here. Remember to define HTTP request authorization settings to achieve that. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--authentication_settings--http--rules--jwt))

<a id="nestedatt--mandates--authentication_settings--http--rules--jwt"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandates.authentication_settings.http.rules.jwt`

Required:

- `issuer` (String) Identifies the issuer that issued the JWT. See [issuer](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7519#section-4.1.1) A JWT with different `iss` claim will be rejected. Example: https://foobar.auth0.com Example: 1234567-compute@developer.gserviceaccount.com

Optional:

- `audiences` (List of String) The list of JWT [audiences](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7519#section-4.1.3). that are allowed to access. A JWT containing any of these audiences will be accepted. The service name will be accepted if audiences is empty.
- `from_cookies` (List of String) List of cookie names from which JWT is expected. For example, if config is:  fromCookies:  - auth-token Then JWT will be extracted from auth-token cookie in the request. Note: Requests with multiple tokens (at different locations) are not supported. The behaviour of authorization policies when there is more than one user identity is undefined
- `from_headers` (Attributes List) This field specifies the locations to extract JWT token. If no explicit location is specified the following default locations are tried in order:     1) The Authorization header using the Bearer schema,        e.g. Authorization: Bearer <token>. (see        [Authorization Request Header        Field](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6750#section-2.1))     2) The `access_token` query parameter (see     [URI Query Parameter](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6750#section-2.3)) List of header locations from which JWT is expected. For example, below is the location spec if JWT is expected to be found in `x-jwt-assertion` header, and have `Bearer ` prefix: ```yaml   fromHeaders:   - name: x-jwt-assertion     prefix: "Bearer " ``` Note: Multiple tokens present on the same request are not supported.  The behaviour of authorization policies when there is more than one user identity is undefined (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--authentication_settings--http--rules--jwt--from_headers))
- `jwks` (String) JSON Web Key Set of public keys to validate signature of the JWT. See https://auth0.com/docs/jwks. Note: Only one of jwks_uri and jwks should be used. jwks_uri will be ignored if it does.
- `jwks_uri` (String) URL of the provider's public key set to validate signature of the JWT. See [OpenID Discovery](https://openid.net/specs/openid-connect-discovery-1_0.html#ProviderMetadata). Optional if the key set document can either (a) be retrieved from [OpenID Discovery](https://openid.net/specs/openid-connect-discovery-1_0.html) of the issuer or (b) inferred from the email domain of the issuer (e.g. a Google service account). Example: `https://www.googleapis.com/oauth2/v1/certs` Note: Only one of jwks_uri and jwks should be used. jwks_uri will be ignored if it does.
- `output_claim_to_headers` (Attributes List) This field specifies a list of operations to copy the claim to HTTP headers on a successfully verified token. This differs from the `output_payload_to_header` by allowing outputting individual claims instead of the whole payload. Only claims of type string, boolean, and integer are supported. Array type claims are not supported at this time. The header specified in each operation in the list must be unique. Nested claims of type string/int/bool is supported as well. ```   outputClaimToHeaders:   - header: x-my-company-jwt-group     claim: my-group   - header: x-test-environment-flag     claim: test-flag   - header: x-jwt-claim-group     claim: nested.key.group ``` [Experimental] This feature is a experimental feature. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--authentication_settings--http--rules--jwt--output_claim_to_headers))
- `output_payload_to_header` (String) This field specifies the header name to output a successfully verified JWT payload to the backend. The forwarded data is `base64_encoded(jwt_payload_in_JSON)`. If it is not specified, the payload will not be emitted.

<a id="nestedatt--mandates--authentication_settings--http--rules--jwt--from_headers"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandates.authentication_settings.http.rules.jwt.from_headers`

Required:

- `name` (String) The HTTP header name.

Optional:

- `prefix` (String) The prefix that should be stripped before decoding the token. For example, for `Authorization: Bearer <token>`, prefix=`Bearer ` with a space at the end. If the header doesn't have this exact prefix, it is considered invalid.


<a id="nestedatt--mandates--authentication_settings--http--rules--jwt--output_claim_to_headers"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandates.authentication_settings.http.rules.jwt.output_claim_to_headers`

Required:

- `claim` (String) The name of the claim to be copied from. Only claim of type string/int/bool is supported. The header will not be there if the claim does not exist or the type of the claim is not supported.
- `header` (String) The name of the header to be created. The header will be overridden if it already exists in the request.






<a id="nestedatt--mandates--traffic"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandates.traffic`

Optional:

- `inbound` (Attributes) Configures inbound traffic. Applicable when service acts as a server. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound))
- `outbound` (Attributes) Configures outbound traffic. Applicable when service acts as a client. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--traffic--outbound))

<a id="nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandates.traffic.inbound`

Optional:

- `failover_settings` (Attributes) Failover settings apply to all clients accessing the hostname defined in this section. While the configuration is set by the user on the server/service side,  TSB ensures that client proxies implement these settings. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--failover_settings))
- `rate_limiting` (Attributes) Configuration for rate limiting requests. Only applies to sidecars in traffic group today. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--rate_limiting))
- `resilience` (Attributes) Resiliency configuration for inbound connections. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--resilience))

<a id="nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--failover_settings"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandates.traffic.inbound.failover_settings`

Optional:

- `automatic_load_balancing` (Attributes) Settings for configuring automatic load balancing between clusters based on observed metrics. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--failover_settings--automatic_load_balancing))
- `failover_priority` (List of String) FailoverPriority specifies the failover priority for traffic. FailoverPriority is an ordered list of labels  used to sort endpoints to do priority based load balancing.  This is to support traffic failover across different groups of endpoints. Internally these labels will be matched on both the client and endpoints to determine the priorities for the respective endpoints based on clients. Note: For a label to be considered for match, the previous labels must match, i.e.  nth label would be considered matched only if first n-1 labels match. If for a particular client-endpoint pair, all the n labels match, the endpoint will be considered P(0).bool If first n-1 labels match, the endpoint will be considered P(1) and so on. For getting the labels to be populated on the endpoints generated by the TSB for multicluster and eastwest scenario,  you will need to label the kubernetes service of your gateway or east-west exposed service using a label with prefix `failover.tetrate.io/`.  For example `failover.tetrate.io/version=v1` should be the label present on the kubernetes service of remote gateway or exposed service for east west traffic. Example of failoverPriority using these labels: ```yaml failoverPriority: - "failover.tetrate.io/version=v1" - "failover.tetrate.io/domain" ``` Another way to label the endpoints for eastwest scenario is to create a ServiceRoute object for the service and specify the labels in the ServiceRoute object. If there is any pod with such label present in the remote cluster, the endpoints for it will have these labels and thus it could be used in failoverPriority API. For example: Suppose if one of your clusters has service reviews only with version v1 and a second cluster with reviews only with version v2, Then use the below serviceroute object to populate service labels to the endpoints dynamically:  ```yaml apiVersion: traffic.tsb.tetrate.io/v2 kind: ServiceRoute metadata:   name: reviews   group: t1   workspace: w1   tenant: mycompany   organization: myorg spec:   service: ns1/reviews.ns1.svc.cluster.local   subsets:   - name: v1     labels:       version: v1   - name: v2     labels:       version: v2 ``` Example of failoverPriority using these labels: ```yaml failoverPriority: - "version=v1" - "failover.tetrate.io/domain" ```
- `regional_failover` (Attributes List) Locality routing settings for all gateways in the Workspace/Organization for which this is defined. Explicitly specify the region traffic will land on when endpoints in the local region become unhealthy. Should be used together with OutlierDetection to detect unhealthy endpoints. Note: if no OutlierDetection specified, this will not take effect. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--failover_settings--regional_failover))
- `topology_choice` (String) TopologyChoice specifies the topology preference for traffic priority. If not specified, the default value is `CLUSTER`. If failoverPriority is specified then this value is ignored.

<a id="nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--failover_settings--automatic_load_balancing"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandates.traffic.inbound.failover_settings.automatic_load_balancing`

Optional:

- `enabled` (Attributes) Whether to enable automatic load balancing. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--failover_settings--automatic_load_balancing--enabled))

<a id="nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--failover_settings--automatic_load_balancing--enabled"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandates.traffic.inbound.failover_settings.automatic_load_balancing.enabled`

Optional:

- `value` (Boolean) The bool value.



<a id="nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--failover_settings--regional_failover"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandates.traffic.inbound.failover_settings.regional_failover`

Optional:

- `from` (String) Originating region.
- `to` (String) Destination region the traffic will fail over to when endpoints in the 'from' region become unhealthy.



<a id="nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--rate_limiting"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandates.traffic.inbound.rate_limiting`

Optional:

- `external_service` (Attributes) Configure ratelimiting using an external ratelimit server. This configuration only configures Envoy's ratelimit filters The user is expected to provision and configure their own external ratelimit server with the appropriate ratelimit values (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--rate_limiting--external_service))
- `local` (Attributes) Apply rate limits internally in the Envoy proxy. Envoy will manage the rate limiting buckets in memory. Each replica will have its own token bucket and apply rate limits independently. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--rate_limiting--local))
- `settings` (Attributes) Use Envoy and TSB's rateLimit server for ratelimiting HTTP Requests (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--rate_limiting--settings))

<a id="nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--rate_limiting--external_service"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandates.traffic.inbound.rate_limiting.external_service`

Required:

- `domain` (String) The rate limit domain to use when calling the rate limit service. Ratelimit settings are namespaced to a domain.
- `rate_limit_server_uri` (String) The URI at which the external rate limit server can be reached.
- `rules` (Attributes List) A set of rate limit rules. Each rule describes a list of dimension to match on. Once matched, a list of descriptors are sent to the external rate limit server (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--rate_limiting--external_service--rules))

Optional:

- `fail_closed` (Boolean) If the rate limit service is unavailable, the request will fail if failClosed is set to true. Defaults to false.
- `timeout` (Attributes) The timeout in seconds for the external rate limit server RPC. Defaults to 0.020 seconds (20ms). Traffic will not be allowed to the destination if failClosed is set to true and the request to the rate limit server times out. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--rate_limiting--external_service--timeout))
- `tls` (Attributes) Configure TLS parameters to be used when connecting to the external rate limit server. By default, the client will not validate the certificates it is presented with. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--rate_limiting--external_service--tls))

<a id="nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--rate_limiting--external_service--rules"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandates.traffic.inbound.rate_limiting.external_service.rules`

Required:

- `dimensions` (Attributes List) A list of dimensions that are to be applied for this rate limit configuration. Order matters as the dimensions are processed sequentially and the descriptor is composed by appending descriptor entries in that sequence. If the condition for a dimension is not satisfied and cannot append a descriptor entry, no descriptor list is generated for the entire setting. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--rate_limiting--external_service--rules--dimensions))

<a id="nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--rate_limiting--external_service--rules--dimensions"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandates.traffic.inbound.rate_limiting.external_service.rules.dimensions`

Optional:

- `destination_cluster` (Attributes) Rate limit on destination envoy cluster. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--rate_limiting--external_service--rules--dimensions--destination_cluster))
- `header_value_match` (Attributes) Rate limit on the existence of certain request headers. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--rate_limiting--external_service--rules--dimensions--header_value_match))
- `remote_address` (Attributes) Rate limit on remote address of client. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--rate_limiting--external_service--rules--dimensions--remote_address))
- `request_headers` (Attributes) Rate limit on the value of certain request headers. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--rate_limiting--external_service--rules--dimensions--request_headers))
- `source_cluster` (Attributes) Rate limit on source envoy cluster. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--rate_limiting--external_service--rules--dimensions--source_cluster))

<a id="nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--rate_limiting--external_service--rules--dimensions--destination_cluster"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandates.traffic.inbound.rate_limiting.external_service.rules.dimensions.destination_cluster`


<a id="nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--rate_limiting--external_service--rules--dimensions--header_value_match"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandates.traffic.inbound.rate_limiting.external_service.rules.dimensions.header_value_match`

Required:

- `descriptor_value` (String) The value to use in the descriptor entry.
- `headers` (Attributes Map) Specifies a set of headers that the rate limit action should match on. The action will check the request’s headers against all the specified headers in the config. A match will happen if all the headers in the config are present in the request with the same values (or based on presence if the value field is not in the config).  The header keys must be lowercase and use hyphen as the separator, e.g. x-request-id. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--rate_limiting--external_service--rules--dimensions--header_value_match--headers))

Optional:

- `dont_match` (Boolean) If set to true, the condition will be met when the header value does not match. Default value is false.

<a id="nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--rate_limiting--external_service--rules--dimensions--header_value_match--headers"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandates.traffic.inbound.rate_limiting.external_service.rules.dimensions.header_value_match.headers`

Optional:

- `exact` (String) Exact string match.
- `prefix` (String) Prefix-based match.
- `regex` (String) ECMAscript style regex-based match.



<a id="nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--rate_limiting--external_service--rules--dimensions--remote_address"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandates.traffic.inbound.rate_limiting.external_service.rules.dimensions.remote_address`


<a id="nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--rate_limiting--external_service--rules--dimensions--request_headers"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandates.traffic.inbound.rate_limiting.external_service.rules.dimensions.request_headers`

Required:

- `descriptor_key` (String) The key to use in the descriptor entry.
- `header_name` (String) The header name to be queried from the request headers. The header’s value is used to populate the value of the descriptor entry for the descriptor_key.


<a id="nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--rate_limiting--external_service--rules--dimensions--source_cluster"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandates.traffic.inbound.rate_limiting.external_service.rules.dimensions.source_cluster`




<a id="nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--rate_limiting--external_service--timeout"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandates.traffic.inbound.rate_limiting.external_service.timeout`

Optional:

- `nanos` (Number) Signed fractions of a second at nanosecond resolution of the span of time. Durations less than one second are represented with a 0 `seconds` field and a positive or negative `nanos` field. For durations of one second or more, a non-zero value for the `nanos` field must be of the same sign as the `seconds` field. Must be from -999,999,999 to +999,999,999 inclusive.
- `seconds` (Number) Signed seconds of the span of time. Must be from -315,576,000,000 to +315,576,000,000 inclusive. Note: these bounds are computed from: 60 sec/min * 60 min/hr * 24 hr/day * 365.25 days/year * 10000 years


<a id="nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--rate_limiting--external_service--tls"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandates.traffic.inbound.rate_limiting.external_service.tls`

Optional:

- `files` (Attributes) TLS key source from files. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--rate_limiting--external_service--tls--files))
- `mode` (String) Set this to DISABLED to disable TLS (not recommended from the security perspective), SIMPLE for one-way TLS and MUTUAL for mutual TLS (where client is required to present its certificate as well)
- `secret_name` (String) TLS key source from a Kubernetes Secret. This is applicable for gateway workloads.
- `subject_alt_names` (List of String) Subject alternative names is the list of names that are accepted as service name as part of TLS handshake

<a id="nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--rate_limiting--external_service--tls--files"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandates.traffic.inbound.rate_limiting.external_service.tls.files`

Optional:

- `ca_certificates` (String) File containing CA certificates to verify the certificates presented by the server. This is mandatory for both simple and mutual TLS. Here are some common paths for the system CA bundle on Linux and can be specified here if the server certificate is signed by a well known authority, already part of the system CA bundle on the host -    /etc/ssl/certs/ca-certificates.crt (Debian/Ubuntu/Gentoo etc.)   /etc/pki/ca-trust/extracted/pem/tls-ca-bundle.pem (CentOS/RHEL 7)   /etc/pki/tls/certs/ca-bundle.crt (Fedora/RHEL 6)
- `client_certificate` (String) Certificate file to authenticate the client. This is mandatory for mutual TLS and must not be specified for simple (one-way) TLS
- `private_key` (String) Private key file associated with the client certificate. This is mandatory for mutual TLS and must not be specified for simple TLS




<a id="nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--rate_limiting--local"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandates.traffic.inbound.rate_limiting.local`

Required:

- `rules` (Attributes List) A list of rules for ratelimiting. Each rule defines a list of dimensions to match on and the rate limit value for the rule. Each rule is independent of the other synonymous to having an OR relationship. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--rate_limiting--local--rules))

Optional:

- `max_wildcard_dimensions` (Attributes) The maximum number of unique values that will be kept for dimensions with a wildcard. This limits memory usage, when this number is reached, the least recently used entry will be dropped. If this is set too low, the rate limits will not apply to all users. Defaults to 20. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--rate_limiting--local--max_wildcard_dimensions))

<a id="nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--rate_limiting--local--rules"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandates.traffic.inbound.rate_limiting.local.rules`

Required:

- `dimensions` (Attributes List) A list of dimensions to define each ratelimit rule. Requests count towards the ratelimit value only when each and every dimension is matched for a given HTTP request synonymous to having an AND relationship. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--rate_limiting--local--rules--dimensions))
- `token_bucket` (Attributes) The ratelimit value that will be configured for the above rules. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--rate_limiting--local--rules--token_bucket))

<a id="nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--rate_limiting--local--rules--dimensions"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandates.traffic.inbound.rate_limiting.local.rules.dimensions`

Optional:

- `header` (Attributes) Rate limit on certain HTTP headers. Special header names such as `:path` and `:method` can also be used. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--rate_limiting--local--rules--dimensions--header))
- `remote_address` (Attributes) Rate limit on the remote address of client. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--rate_limiting--local--rules--dimensions--remote_address))

<a id="nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--rate_limiting--local--rules--dimensions--header"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandates.traffic.inbound.rate_limiting.local.rules.dimensions.header`

Required:

- `name` (Attributes) Name of the header to match on. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--rate_limiting--local--rules--dimensions--header--name))

Optional:

- `dont_match` (Attributes) If set to true, the condition will be met when the header value does not match. Default value is false. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--rate_limiting--local--rules--dimensions--header--dont_match))
- `value` (Attributes) Value of the header to match on if matching on a specific value. If not specified, ratelimit on every unique value of the header. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--rate_limiting--local--rules--dimensions--header--value))

<a id="nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--rate_limiting--local--rules--dimensions--header--name"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandates.traffic.inbound.rate_limiting.local.rules.dimensions.header.name`

Optional:

- `value` (String) The string value.


<a id="nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--rate_limiting--local--rules--dimensions--header--dont_match"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandates.traffic.inbound.rate_limiting.local.rules.dimensions.header.dont_match`

Optional:

- `value` (Boolean) The bool value.


<a id="nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--rate_limiting--local--rules--dimensions--header--value"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandates.traffic.inbound.rate_limiting.local.rules.dimensions.header.value`

Optional:

- `exact` (String) Exact string match.
- `prefix` (String) Prefix-based match.
- `regex` (String) ECMAscript style regex-based match.



<a id="nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--rate_limiting--local--rules--dimensions--remote_address"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandates.traffic.inbound.rate_limiting.local.rules.dimensions.remote_address`

Required:

- `value` (Attributes) Ratelimit on a specific remote address. If the value is set to "*", ratelimit on every unique remote address. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--rate_limiting--local--rules--dimensions--remote_address--value))

<a id="nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--rate_limiting--local--rules--dimensions--remote_address--value"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandates.traffic.inbound.rate_limiting.local.rules.dimensions.remote_address.value`

Optional:

- `value` (String) The string value.




<a id="nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--rate_limiting--local--rules--token_bucket"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandates.traffic.inbound.rate_limiting.local.rules.token_bucket`

Required:

- `fill_interval` (Attributes) The fill interval that tokens are added to the bucket. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--rate_limiting--local--rules--token_bucket--fill_interval))
- `max_tokens` (Attributes) The maximum tokens that the bucket can hold. This is also the number of tokens that the bucket initially contains. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--rate_limiting--local--rules--token_bucket--max_tokens))

Optional:

- `tokens_per_fill` (Attributes) The number of tokens added to the bucket during each fill interval. If not specified, defaults to a single token. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--rate_limiting--local--rules--token_bucket--tokens_per_fill))

<a id="nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--rate_limiting--local--rules--token_bucket--fill_interval"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandates.traffic.inbound.rate_limiting.local.rules.token_bucket.fill_interval`

Optional:

- `nanos` (Number) Signed fractions of a second at nanosecond resolution of the span of time. Durations less than one second are represented with a 0 `seconds` field and a positive or negative `nanos` field. For durations of one second or more, a non-zero value for the `nanos` field must be of the same sign as the `seconds` field. Must be from -999,999,999 to +999,999,999 inclusive.
- `seconds` (Number) Signed seconds of the span of time. Must be from -315,576,000,000 to +315,576,000,000 inclusive. Note: these bounds are computed from: 60 sec/min * 60 min/hr * 24 hr/day * 365.25 days/year * 10000 years


<a id="nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--rate_limiting--local--rules--token_bucket--max_tokens"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandates.traffic.inbound.rate_limiting.local.rules.token_bucket.max_tokens`

Optional:

- `value` (Number) The uint32 value.


<a id="nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--rate_limiting--local--rules--token_bucket--tokens_per_fill"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandates.traffic.inbound.rate_limiting.local.rules.token_bucket.tokens_per_fill`

Optional:

- `value` (Number) The uint32 value.




<a id="nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--rate_limiting--local--max_wildcard_dimensions"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandates.traffic.inbound.rate_limiting.local.max_wildcard_dimensions`

Optional:

- `value` (Number) The uint32 value.



<a id="nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--rate_limiting--settings"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandates.traffic.inbound.rate_limiting.settings`

Required:

- `rules` (Attributes List) A list of rules for ratelimiting. Each rule defines a list of dimensions to match on and the rate limit value for the rule. Each rule is independant of the other. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--rate_limiting--settings--rules))

Optional:

- `fail_closed` (Boolean) If the rate limit service is unavailable, the request will fail if failClosed is set to true. Defaults to false.
- `timeout` (Attributes) The timeout in seconds for the rate limit server RPC. Defaults to 0.020 seconds (20ms). Traffic will not be allowed to the destination if failClosed is set to true and the request to the rate limit server times out. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--rate_limiting--settings--timeout))

<a id="nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--rate_limiting--settings--rules"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandates.traffic.inbound.rate_limiting.settings.rules`

Required:

- `dimensions` (Attributes List) A list of dimensions to define each ratelimit rule. Requests count towards the ratelimit value only when each and every condition in a dimension is matched for a given HTTP request. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--rate_limiting--settings--rules--dimensions))
- `limit` (Attributes) The ratelimit value that will be configured for the above rules. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--rate_limiting--settings--rules--limit))

<a id="nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--rate_limiting--settings--rules--dimensions"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandates.traffic.inbound.rate_limiting.settings.rules.dimensions`

Optional:

- `header` (Attributes) Rate limit on certain HTTP headers. Special header names such as `:path` and `:method` can also be used. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--rate_limiting--settings--rules--dimensions--header))
- `remote_address` (Attributes) Rate limit on the remote address of client. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--rate_limiting--settings--rules--dimensions--remote_address))

<a id="nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--rate_limiting--settings--rules--dimensions--header"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandates.traffic.inbound.rate_limiting.settings.rules.dimensions.header`

Required:

- `name` (String) Name of the header to match on.

Optional:

- `dont_match` (Boolean) If set to true, the condition will be met when the header value does not match. Default value is false.
- `value` (Attributes) Value of the header to match on if matching on a specific value. If not specified, ratelimit on every unique value of the header. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--rate_limiting--settings--rules--dimensions--header--value))

<a id="nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--rate_limiting--settings--rules--dimensions--header--value"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandates.traffic.inbound.rate_limiting.settings.rules.dimensions.header.value`

Optional:

- `exact` (String) Exact string match.
- `prefix` (String) Prefix-based match.
- `regex` (String) ECMAscript style regex-based match.



<a id="nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--rate_limiting--settings--rules--dimensions--remote_address"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandates.traffic.inbound.rate_limiting.settings.rules.dimensions.remote_address`

Required:

- `value` (String) Ratelimit on a specific remote address. If the value is set to "*", ratelimit on every unique remote address.



<a id="nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--rate_limiting--settings--rules--limit"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandates.traffic.inbound.rate_limiting.settings.rules.limit`

Required:

- `requests_per_unit` (Number) Specifies the value of the rate limit.
- `unit` (String) Specifies the unit of time for rate limit.



<a id="nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--rate_limiting--settings--timeout"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandates.traffic.inbound.rate_limiting.settings.timeout`

Optional:

- `nanos` (Number) Signed fractions of a second at nanosecond resolution of the span of time. Durations less than one second are represented with a 0 `seconds` field and a positive or negative `nanos` field. For durations of one second or more, a non-zero value for the `nanos` field must be of the same sign as the `seconds` field. Must be from -999,999,999 to +999,999,999 inclusive.
- `seconds` (Number) Signed seconds of the span of time. Must be from -315,576,000,000 to +315,576,000,000 inclusive. Note: these bounds are computed from: 60 sec/min * 60 min/hr * 24 hr/day * 365.25 days/year * 10000 years




<a id="nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--resilience"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandates.traffic.inbound.resilience`

Optional:

- `connection_pool` (Attributes) Configures tolerance and other settings for TCP/HTTP connections to the service. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--resilience--connection_pool))
- `mesh_timeout` (Attributes) Configures the max connection and stream durations for HTTP and TCP connections. This applies to the inbound connections at the Sidecars and Gateways coming from a mesh-internal service. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--resilience--mesh_timeout))

<a id="nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--resilience--connection_pool"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandates.traffic.inbound.resilience.connection_pool`

Optional:

- `tcp` (Attributes) TCP connection pool settings (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--resilience--connection_pool--tcp))

<a id="nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--resilience--connection_pool--tcp"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandates.traffic.inbound.resilience.connection_pool.tcp`

Optional:

- `keep_alive` (Attributes) Keep Alive Settings. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--resilience--connection_pool--tcp--keep_alive))

<a id="nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--resilience--connection_pool--tcp--keep_alive"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandates.traffic.inbound.resilience.connection_pool.tcp.keep_alive`

Optional:

- `idle_time` (Attributes) The number of seconds a connection needs to be idle before keep-alive probes start being sent. Default is to use the OS level configuration, Linux defaults to 7200s. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--resilience--connection_pool--tcp--keep_alive--idle_time))
- `interval` (Attributes) The number of seconds between keep-alive probes. Default is to use the OS level configuration, Linux defaults to 75s. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--resilience--connection_pool--tcp--keep_alive--interval))
- `probes` (Attributes) The total number of unacknowledged probes to send before deciding the connection is dead. Default is to use the OS level configuration, Linux defaults to 9. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--resilience--connection_pool--tcp--keep_alive--probes))

<a id="nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--resilience--connection_pool--tcp--keep_alive--idle_time"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandates.traffic.inbound.resilience.connection_pool.tcp.keep_alive.idle_time`

Optional:

- `value` (Number) The uint32 value.


<a id="nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--resilience--connection_pool--tcp--keep_alive--interval"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandates.traffic.inbound.resilience.connection_pool.tcp.keep_alive.interval`

Optional:

- `value` (Number) The uint32 value.


<a id="nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--resilience--connection_pool--tcp--keep_alive--probes"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandates.traffic.inbound.resilience.connection_pool.tcp.keep_alive.probes`

Optional:

- `value` (Number) The uint32 value.





<a id="nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--resilience--mesh_timeout"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandates.traffic.inbound.resilience.mesh_timeout`

Optional:

- `max_connection_duration` (Attributes) This specifies the duration of time after which a downstream and upstream connection will be drained and/or closed, starting from when it was first established. If there are no active streams, the connection will be closed. If there are any active streams, the drain sequence will kick-in, and the connection will be force-closed after the drain period. The default value of max connection duration is 0 or unlimited, which means that the connections will never be closed due to aging. This setting applies to the entire HTTP connection and all streams (HTTP/2 and HTTP/3) the connection carries. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--resilience--mesh_timeout--max_connection_duration))
- `max_downstream_connection_duration` (Attributes) The maximum duration of a TCP connection. The duration is defined as the period since a connection was established. If not set, there is no max duration. When max_downstream_connection_duration is reached the connection will be closed. This can be used alongside with `max_connection_duration`. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--resilience--mesh_timeout--max_downstream_connection_duration))
- `max_stream_duration` (Attributes) The max stream duration is the maximum time that a stream’s lifetime will span. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--resilience--mesh_timeout--max_stream_duration))
- `proxy_type` (String) Specifies the type of proxy to which to apply the mesh timeout settings. The default is to apply the settings to both Gateways and Sidecars.

<a id="nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--resilience--mesh_timeout--max_connection_duration"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandates.traffic.inbound.resilience.mesh_timeout.max_connection_duration`

Optional:

- `nanos` (Number) Signed fractions of a second at nanosecond resolution of the span of time. Durations less than one second are represented with a 0 `seconds` field and a positive or negative `nanos` field. For durations of one second or more, a non-zero value for the `nanos` field must be of the same sign as the `seconds` field. Must be from -999,999,999 to +999,999,999 inclusive.
- `seconds` (Number) Signed seconds of the span of time. Must be from -315,576,000,000 to +315,576,000,000 inclusive. Note: these bounds are computed from: 60 sec/min * 60 min/hr * 24 hr/day * 365.25 days/year * 10000 years


<a id="nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--resilience--mesh_timeout--max_downstream_connection_duration"></a>

Optional:

- `nanos` (Number) Signed fractions of a second at nanosecond resolution of the span of time. Durations less than one second are represented with a 0 `seconds` field and a positive or negative `nanos` field. For durations of one second or more, a non-zero value for the `nanos` field must be of the same sign as the `seconds` field. Must be from -999,999,999 to +999,999,999 inclusive.
- `seconds` (Number) Signed seconds of the span of time. Must be from -315,576,000,000 to +315,576,000,000 inclusive. Note: these bounds are computed from: 60 sec/min * 60 min/hr * 24 hr/day * 365.25 days/year * 10000 years


<a id="nestedatt--mandates--traffic--inbound--resilience--mesh_timeout--max_stream_duration"></a>
