Optional:

- `nanos` (Number) Signed fractions of a second at nanosecond resolution of the span of time. Durations less than one second are represented with a 0 `seconds` field and a positive or negative `nanos` field. For durations of one second or more, a non-zero value for the `nanos` field must be of the same sign as the `seconds` field. Must be from -999,999,999 to +999,999,999 inclusive.
- `seconds` (Number) Signed seconds of the span of time. Must be from -315,576,000,000 to +315,576,000,000 inclusive. Note: these bounds are computed from: 60 sec/min * 60 min/hr * 24 hr/day * 365.25 days/year * 10000 years





<a id="nestedatt--mandates--traffic--outbound"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandates.traffic.outbound`

Optional:

- `egress` (Attributes) Specifies the details of the egress proxy to which traffic to services that are not part to the mesh should be forwarded to from the proxy workloads. If not specified, the proxy workloads will send this traffic directly to the IP requested by the application. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--traffic--outbound--egress))
- `reachability` (Attributes) The set of services and hosts accessed by a workload (and hence its sidecar) in the mesh. Defining the set of services accessed by a workload (i.e. its dependencies) in advance reduces the memory and CPU consumption both the Istio control plane and the individual Envoy proxy workloads in the data plane. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--traffic--outbound--reachability))
- `upstream_traffic_settings` (Attributes List) List of hosts and the associated traffic settings to be used by the clients sending traffic to them. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--traffic--outbound--upstream_traffic_settings))

<a id="nestedatt--mandates--traffic--outbound--egress"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandates.traffic.outbound.egress`

Required:

- `host` (String) Specifies the egress gateway hostname. Must be in `<namespace>/<fqdn>` format.


<a id="nestedatt--mandates--traffic--outbound--reachability"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandates.traffic.outbound.reachability`

Optional:

- `hosts` (List of String) When the mode is `CUSTOM`, `hosts` specify the set of services that the sidecar should be able to reach. Must be in the `<namespace>/<fqdn>` format. - `./*` indicates all services in the namespace where the sidecar resides. - `ns1/*` indicates all services in the `ns1` namespace. - `ns1/svc1.com` indicates `svc1.com` service in `ns1` namespace. - `*/svc1.com` indicates `svc1.com` service in any namespace.
- `mode` (String) A short cut for specifying the set of services accessed by the workload.


<a id="nestedatt--mandates--traffic--outbound--upstream_traffic_settings"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandates.traffic.outbound.upstream_traffic_settings`

Optional:

- `hosts` (List of String) List of hosts for which the settings will be created. Can contain wildcard hosts. The host should be a service from the service registry or a host declared by ServiceEntries.
- `settings` (Attributes) A single setting to be applied to all the clients connecting to the upstream hosts. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--traffic--outbound--upstream_traffic_settings--settings))

<a id="nestedatt--mandates--traffic--outbound--upstream_traffic_settings--settings"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandates.traffic.outbound.upstream_traffic_settings.settings`

Optional:

- `authentication` (Attributes) Configuration for connection authentication parameters. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--traffic--outbound--upstream_traffic_settings--settings--authentication))
- `load_balancer` (Attributes) Load balancing settings for the clients. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--traffic--outbound--upstream_traffic_settings--settings--load_balancer))
- `resilience` (Attributes) Resilience settings for the clients. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--traffic--outbound--upstream_traffic_settings--settings--resilience))

<a id="nestedatt--mandates--traffic--outbound--upstream_traffic_settings--settings--authentication"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandates.traffic.outbound.upstream_traffic_settings.settings.authentication`

Optional:

- `traffic_mode` (String) If set to `REQUIRED`, client sidecars under this configuration will be configured to initiate mTLS connections using mesh-generated client certificates to services that do not have a sidecar injected.


<a id="nestedatt--mandates--traffic--outbound--upstream_traffic_settings--settings--load_balancer"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandates.traffic.outbound.upstream_traffic_settings.settings.load_balancer`

Optional:

- `consistent_hash` (Attributes) Use consistent hash load balancing which can provide soft session affinity. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--traffic--outbound--upstream_traffic_settings--settings--load_balancer--consistent_hash))
- `simple` (String) Use standard load balancing algorithms that require no tuning.

<a id="nestedatt--mandates--traffic--outbound--upstream_traffic_settings--settings--load_balancer--consistent_hash"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandates.traffic.outbound.upstream_traffic_settings.settings.load_balancer.consistent_hash`

Optional:

- `http_cookie` (Attributes) Hash based on HTTP cookie. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--traffic--outbound--upstream_traffic_settings--settings--load_balancer--consistent_hash--http_cookie))
- `http_header_name` (String) Hash based on a specific HTTP header.
- `http_query_parameter_name` (String) Hash based on a specific HTTP query parameter.
- `maglev` (Attributes) The Maglev load balancer implements consistent hashing to backend hosts. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--traffic--outbound--upstream_traffic_settings--settings--load_balancer--consistent_hash--maglev))
- `ring_hash` (Attributes) The ring/modulo hash load balancer implements consistent hashing to backend hosts. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--traffic--outbound--upstream_traffic_settings--settings--load_balancer--consistent_hash--ring_hash))
- `use_source_ip` (Boolean) Hash based on the source IP address. This is applicable for both TCP and HTTP connections.

<a id="nestedatt--mandates--traffic--outbound--upstream_traffic_settings--settings--load_balancer--consistent_hash--http_cookie"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandates.traffic.outbound.upstream_traffic_settings.settings.load_balancer.consistent_hash.http_cookie`

Required:

- `name` (String) Name of the cookie.
- `ttl` (Attributes) Lifetime of the cookie. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--traffic--outbound--upstream_traffic_settings--settings--load_balancer--consistent_hash--http_cookie--ttl))

Optional:

- `path` (String) Path to set for the cookie.

<a id="nestedatt--mandates--traffic--outbound--upstream_traffic_settings--settings--load_balancer--consistent_hash--http_cookie--ttl"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandates.traffic.outbound.upstream_traffic_settings.settings.load_balancer.consistent_hash.http_cookie.ttl`

Optional:

- `nanos` (Number) Signed fractions of a second at nanosecond resolution of the span of time. Durations less than one second are represented with a 0 `seconds` field and a positive or negative `nanos` field. For durations of one second or more, a non-zero value for the `nanos` field must be of the same sign as the `seconds` field. Must be from -999,999,999 to +999,999,999 inclusive.
- `seconds` (Number) Signed seconds of the span of time. Must be from -315,576,000,000 to +315,576,000,000 inclusive. Note: these bounds are computed from: 60 sec/min * 60 min/hr * 24 hr/day * 365.25 days/year * 10000 years



<a id="nestedatt--mandates--traffic--outbound--upstream_traffic_settings--settings--load_balancer--consistent_hash--maglev"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandates.traffic.outbound.upstream_traffic_settings.settings.load_balancer.consistent_hash.maglev`

Required:

- `table_size` (Number) The table size for Maglev hashing. This helps in controlling the disruption when the backend hosts change. Increasing the table size reduces the amount of disruption.


<a id="nestedatt--mandates--traffic--outbound--upstream_traffic_settings--settings--load_balancer--consistent_hash--ring_hash"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandates.traffic.outbound.upstream_traffic_settings.settings.load_balancer.consistent_hash.ring_hash`

Optional:

- `minimum_ring_size` (Number) The minimum number of virtual nodes to use for the hash ring. Defaults to 1024. Larger ring sizes result in more granular load distributions. If the number of hosts in the load balancing pool is larger than the ring size, each host will be assigned a single virtual node.




<a id="nestedatt--mandates--traffic--outbound--upstream_traffic_settings--settings--resilience"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandates.traffic.outbound.upstream_traffic_settings.settings.resilience`

Optional:

- `circuit_breaker_sensitivity` (String) Circuit breakers in Envoy are applied per endpoint in a load balancing pool. By default, circuit breakers are disabled. If set, the sensitivity level determines the maximum number of consecutive failures that Envoy will tolerate before ejecting an endpoint from the load balancing pool.
- `connection_pool` (Attributes) Configures tolerance and other settings for TCP/HTTP connections to the service. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--traffic--outbound--upstream_traffic_settings--settings--resilience--connection_pool))
- `outlier_detection` (Attributes) Outlier detection settings for the upstream host when custom mode is used. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--traffic--outbound--upstream_traffic_settings--settings--resilience--outlier_detection))

<a id="nestedatt--mandates--traffic--outbound--upstream_traffic_settings--settings--resilience--connection_pool"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandates.traffic.outbound.upstream_traffic_settings.settings.resilience.connection_pool`

Optional:

- `http` (Attributes) HTTP connection pool settings (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--traffic--outbound--upstream_traffic_settings--settings--resilience--connection_pool--http))
- `tcp` (Attributes) TCP connection pool settings (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--traffic--outbound--upstream_traffic_settings--settings--resilience--connection_pool--tcp))

<a id="nestedatt--mandates--traffic--outbound--upstream_traffic_settings--settings--resilience--connection_pool--http"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandates.traffic.outbound.upstream_traffic_settings.settings.resilience.connection_pool.http`

Optional:

- `max_requests` (Number) Maximum number of active requests to the service. Applicable to both HTTP/1.1 and HTTP2. Default 0, meaning "unlimited", up to 2^32 - 1.
- `max_requests_per_connection` (Number) Maximum number of requests per connection to the service. If set to 1, it disables keep alive. Default 0, meaning "unlimited", up to 2^29.
- `request_timeout` (Attributes) Timeout for HTTP requests. format: 1h/1m/1s/1ms. MUST BE >=1ms. Disabled if not set. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--traffic--outbound--upstream_traffic_settings--settings--resilience--connection_pool--http--request_timeout))
- `retries` (Attributes) Retry policy for HTTP requests. Disabled if not set. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--traffic--outbound--upstream_traffic_settings--settings--resilience--connection_pool--http--retries))

<a id="nestedatt--mandates--traffic--outbound--upstream_traffic_settings--settings--resilience--connection_pool--http--request_timeout"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandates.traffic.outbound.upstream_traffic_settings.settings.resilience.connection_pool.http.request_timeout`

Optional:

- `nanos` (Number) Signed fractions of a second at nanosecond resolution of the span of time. Durations less than one second are represented with a 0 `seconds` field and a positive or negative `nanos` field. For durations of one second or more, a non-zero value for the `nanos` field must be of the same sign as the `seconds` field. Must be from -999,999,999 to +999,999,999 inclusive.
- `seconds` (Number) Signed seconds of the span of time. Must be from -315,576,000,000 to +315,576,000,000 inclusive. Note: these bounds are computed from: 60 sec/min * 60 min/hr * 24 hr/day * 365.25 days/year * 10000 years


<a id="nestedatt--mandates--traffic--outbound--upstream_traffic_settings--settings--resilience--connection_pool--http--retries"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandates.traffic.outbound.upstream_traffic_settings.settings.resilience.connection_pool.http.retries`

Required:

- `attempts` (Number) Number of retries for a given request. The interval between retries will be determined automatically (25ms+). Actual number of retries attempted depends on the httpReqTimeout.

Optional:

- `per_try_timeout` (Attributes) Timeout per retry attempt for a given request. format: 1h/1m/1s/1ms. MUST BE >=1ms. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--traffic--outbound--upstream_traffic_settings--settings--resilience--connection_pool--http--retries--per_try_timeout))
- `retry_back_off` (Attributes) Specifies the parameters that controls the interval between retry attempts, i.e., how soon the next retry should be attempted after a failure. The back-off interval for the N-th retry is chosen randomly in the range: [0, min((2^N - 1)* B, max_interval)] where B is the base_interval. The interval increases exponentially with each attempt, but is capped at max_interval. Example (B = 25ms, max_interval = 250ms): 1st retry: randomly delayed between 0–24ms 2nd retry: randomly delayed between 0–74ms 3rd retry: randomly delayed between 0–174ms …and so on. Maximum delay is capped at 250ms. This field is optional. If unset, the default base_interval is 25ms which is sufficient for most fast-fail retry scenarios. The default value of max_interval is 10 times the base_interval. You may want to configure this value specific to your retry strategy, for example: - Retries occur before Envoy finishes ejecting a failed endpoint, causing wasted attempts. A larger backoff helps avoid this. - The backend is slow or rate-limited and needs more recovery time between requests. See [envoy retry algorithm](https://www.envoyproxy.io/docs/envoy/latest/configuration/http/http_filters/router_filter#config-http-filters-router-x-envoy-max-retries) for more details. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--traffic--outbound--upstream_traffic_settings--settings--resilience--connection_pool--http--retries--retry_back_off))
- `retry_on` (String) Specifies the conditions under which retry takes place. One or more policies can be specified using a ‘,’ delimited list. See the [retry policies](https://www.envoyproxy.io/docs/envoy/latest/configuration/http/http_filters/router_filter#x-envoy-retry-on) and [gRPC retry policies](https://www.envoyproxy.io/docs/envoy/latest/configuration/http/http_filters/router_filter#x-envoy-retry-grpc-on) for more details.

<a id="nestedatt--mandates--traffic--outbound--upstream_traffic_settings--settings--resilience--connection_pool--http--retries--per_try_timeout"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandates.traffic.outbound.upstream_traffic_settings.settings.resilience.connection_pool.http.retries.per_try_timeout`

Optional:

- `nanos` (Number) Signed fractions of a second at nanosecond resolution of the span of time. Durations less than one second are represented with a 0 `seconds` field and a positive or negative `nanos` field. For durations of one second or more, a non-zero value for the `nanos` field must be of the same sign as the `seconds` field. Must be from -999,999,999 to +999,999,999 inclusive.
- `seconds` (Number) Signed seconds of the span of time. Must be from -315,576,000,000 to +315,576,000,000 inclusive. Note: these bounds are computed from: 60 sec/min * 60 min/hr * 24 hr/day * 365.25 days/year * 10000 years


<a id="nestedatt--mandates--traffic--outbound--upstream_traffic_settings--settings--resilience--connection_pool--http--retries--retry_back_off"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandates.traffic.outbound.upstream_traffic_settings.settings.resilience.connection_pool.http.retries.retry_back_off`

Required:

- `base_interval` (Attributes) The base interval between retry attempts. This parameter is required and must be greater than zero. Values less than 1 ms are rounded up to 1 ms. The default value is 25ms. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--traffic--outbound--upstream_traffic_settings--settings--resilience--connection_pool--http--retries--retry_back_off--base_interval))

Optional:

- `max_interval` (Attributes) The maximum interval between retry attempts. This parameter is optional but must be greater than or equal to base_interval if set. The default is 10 times the base_interval. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--traffic--outbound--upstream_traffic_settings--settings--resilience--connection_pool--http--retries--retry_back_off--max_interval))

<a id="nestedatt--mandates--traffic--outbound--upstream_traffic_settings--settings--resilience--connection_pool--http--retries--retry_back_off--base_interval"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandates.traffic.outbound.upstream_traffic_settings.settings.resilience.connection_pool.http.retries.retry_back_off.base_interval`

Optional:

- `nanos` (Number) Signed fractions of a second at nanosecond resolution of the span of time. Durations less than one second are represented with a 0 `seconds` field and a positive or negative `nanos` field. For durations of one second or more, a non-zero value for the `nanos` field must be of the same sign as the `seconds` field. Must be from -999,999,999 to +999,999,999 inclusive.
- `seconds` (Number) Signed seconds of the span of time. Must be from -315,576,000,000 to +315,576,000,000 inclusive. Note: these bounds are computed from: 60 sec/min * 60 min/hr * 24 hr/day * 365.25 days/year * 10000 years


<a id="nestedatt--mandates--traffic--outbound--upstream_traffic_settings--settings--resilience--connection_pool--http--retries--retry_back_off--max_interval"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandates.traffic.outbound.upstream_traffic_settings.settings.resilience.connection_pool.http.retries.retry_back_off.max_interval`

Optional:

- `nanos` (Number) Signed fractions of a second at nanosecond resolution of the span of time. Durations less than one second are represented with a 0 `seconds` field and a positive or negative `nanos` field. For durations of one second or more, a non-zero value for the `nanos` field must be of the same sign as the `seconds` field. Must be from -999,999,999 to +999,999,999 inclusive.
- `seconds` (Number) Signed seconds of the span of time. Must be from -315,576,000,000 to +315,576,000,000 inclusive. Note: these bounds are computed from: 60 sec/min * 60 min/hr * 24 hr/day * 365.25 days/year * 10000 years





<a id="nestedatt--mandates--traffic--outbound--upstream_traffic_settings--settings--resilience--connection_pool--tcp"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandates.traffic.outbound.upstream_traffic_settings.settings.resilience.connection_pool.tcp`

Optional:

- `connect_timeout` (Attributes) TCP connection timeout. format: 1h/1m/1s/1ms. MUST BE >=1ms. Default is 10s. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--traffic--outbound--upstream_traffic_settings--settings--resilience--connection_pool--tcp--connect_timeout))
- `keep_alive` (Attributes) Keep Alive Settings. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--traffic--outbound--upstream_traffic_settings--settings--resilience--connection_pool--tcp--keep_alive))
- `max_connections` (Number) Maximum number of HTTP1 /TCP connections to the service. Default 0, meaning "unlimited", up to 2^32 - 1.

<a id="nestedatt--mandates--traffic--outbound--upstream_traffic_settings--settings--resilience--connection_pool--tcp--connect_timeout"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandates.traffic.outbound.upstream_traffic_settings.settings.resilience.connection_pool.tcp.connect_timeout`

Optional:

- `nanos` (Number) Signed fractions of a second at nanosecond resolution of the span of time. Durations less than one second are represented with a 0 `seconds` field and a positive or negative `nanos` field. For durations of one second or more, a non-zero value for the `nanos` field must be of the same sign as the `seconds` field. Must be from -999,999,999 to +999,999,999 inclusive.
- `seconds` (Number) Signed seconds of the span of time. Must be from -315,576,000,000 to +315,576,000,000 inclusive. Note: these bounds are computed from: 60 sec/min * 60 min/hr * 24 hr/day * 365.25 days/year * 10000 years


<a id="nestedatt--mandates--traffic--outbound--upstream_traffic_settings--settings--resilience--connection_pool--tcp--keep_alive"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandates.traffic.outbound.upstream_traffic_settings.settings.resilience.connection_pool.tcp.keep_alive`

Optional:

- `idle_time` (Attributes) The number of seconds a connection needs to be idle before keep-alive probes start being sent. Default is to use the OS level configuration, Linux defaults to 7200s. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--traffic--outbound--upstream_traffic_settings--settings--resilience--connection_pool--tcp--keep_alive--idle_time))
- `interval` (Attributes) The number of seconds between keep-alive probes. Default is to use the OS level configuration, Linux defaults to 75s. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--traffic--outbound--upstream_traffic_settings--settings--resilience--connection_pool--tcp--keep_alive--interval))
- `probes` (Attributes) The total number of unacknowledged probes to send before deciding the connection is dead. Default is to use the OS level configuration, Linux defaults to 9. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--traffic--outbound--upstream_traffic_settings--settings--resilience--connection_pool--tcp--keep_alive--probes))

<a id="nestedatt--mandates--traffic--outbound--upstream_traffic_settings--settings--resilience--connection_pool--tcp--keep_alive--idle_time"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandates.traffic.outbound.upstream_traffic_settings.settings.resilience.connection_pool.tcp.keep_alive.idle_time`

Optional:

- `value` (Number) The uint32 value.

### Nested Schema for `mandates.traffic.outbound.upstream_traffic_settings.settings.resilience.connection_pool.tcp.keep_alive.interval`

Optional:

- `value` (Number) The uint32 value.


<a id="nestedatt--mandates--traffic--outbound--upstream_traffic_settings--settings--resilience--connection_pool--tcp--keep_alive--probes"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandates.traffic.outbound.upstream_traffic_settings.settings.resilience.connection_pool.tcp.keep_alive.probes`

Optional:

- `value` (Number) The uint32 value.





<a id="nestedatt--mandates--traffic--outbound--upstream_traffic_settings--settings--resilience--outlier_detection"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandates.traffic.outbound.upstream_traffic_settings.settings.resilience.outlier_detection`

Optional:

- `base_ejection_time` (Attributes) The base time that a host is ejected for. The real time is equal to the base time multiplied by the number of times the host has been ejected. Defaults to 30000ms or 30s. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--traffic--outbound--upstream_traffic_settings--settings--resilience--outlier_detection--base_ejection_time))
- `consecutive_5_xx` (Attributes) The number of consecutive server-side error responses (for HTTP traffic, 5xx responses; for TCP traffic, connection failures; for Redis, failure to respond PONG; etc.) before a consecutive 5xx ejection occurs. Defaults to 5. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--traffic--outbound--upstream_traffic_settings--settings--resilience--outlier_detection--consecutive_5_xx))
- `consecutive_gateway_failure` (Attributes) The number of consecutive gateway failures (502, 503, 504 status codes) before a consecutive gateway failure ejection occurs. Defaults to circuitBreakerSensitivity of MEDIUM(5) in TSB. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--traffic--outbound--upstream_traffic_settings--settings--resilience--outlier_detection--consecutive_gateway_failure))
- `consecutive_local_origin_failure` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--traffic--outbound--upstream_traffic_settings--settings--resilience--outlier_detection--consecutive_local_origin_failure))
- `enforcing_consecutive_5_xx` (Attributes) The percentage of a host to be actually ejected when an outlier status is detected through consecutive 5xx. This setting can be used to disable ejection or to ramp it up slowly. Defaults to 0 in TSB. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--traffic--outbound--upstream_traffic_settings--settings--resilience--outlier_detection--enforcing_consecutive_5_xx))
- `enforcing_consecutive_gateway_failure` (Attributes) The percentage of a host to be ejected when an outlier status is detected through consecutive gateway failures. This setting can be used to disable ejection or to ramp it up slowly. Defaults to 100 in TSB. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--traffic--outbound--upstream_traffic_settings--settings--resilience--outlier_detection--enforcing_consecutive_gateway_failure))
- `enforcing_consecutive_local_origin_failure` (Attributes) The percentage of a host to be actually ejected when an outlier status is detected through consecutive locally originated failures. This setting can be used to disable ejection or to ramp it up slowly. Defaults to 100. Parameter takes effect only when splitExternalLocalOriginErrors is set to true. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--traffic--outbound--upstream_traffic_settings--settings--resilience--outlier_detection--enforcing_consecutive_local_origin_failure))
- `interval` (Attributes) The time interval between ejection analysis sweeps. This can result in both new ejections as well as hosts being returned to service. Defaults to 10000ms or 10s. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--traffic--outbound--upstream_traffic_settings--settings--resilience--outlier_detection--interval))
- `max_ejection_percent` (Attributes) The maximum % of an upstream cluster that can be ejected due to outlier detection. Defaults to 100%. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--traffic--outbound--upstream_traffic_settings--settings--resilience--outlier_detection--max_ejection_percent))
- `max_ejection_time` (Attributes) The maximum time that a host is ejected for. If not specified, the default value (300000ms or 300s) or baseEjectionTime value is applied, whatever is larger. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mandates--traffic--outbound--upstream_traffic_settings--settings--resilience--outlier_detection--max_ejection_time))
- `split_external_local_origin_errors` (Boolean) Determines whether to distinguish local origin failures from external errors. Local Origin Failures are errors that occur within the Envoy process itself, before the request is actually sent to the upstream host. example of these are connection timeout, TCP reset etc. External errors are errors that occur after the request is sent to the upstream host. example of these are 5xx errors, connection refused etc. If set to true, consecutiveLocalOriginFailure and enforcingConsecutiveLocalOriginFailure will be taken into account. Defaults to false.

<a id="nestedatt--mandates--traffic--outbound--upstream_traffic_settings--settings--resilience--outlier_detection--base_ejection_time"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mandates.traffic.outbound.upstream_traffic_settings.settings.resilience.outlier_detection.base_ejection_time`

Optional:

- `nanos` (Number) Signed fractions of a second at nanosecond resolution of the span of time. Durations less than one second are represented with a 0 `seconds` field and a positive or negative `nanos` field. For durations of one second or more, a non-zero value for the `nanos` field must be of the same sign as the `seconds` field. Must be from -999,999,999 to +999,999,999 inclusive.
- `seconds` (Number) Signed seconds of the span of time. Must be from -315,576,000,000 to +315,576,000,000 inclusive. Note: these bounds are computed from: 60 sec/min * 60 min/hr * 24 hr/day * 365.25 days/year * 10000 years

- `nanos` (Number) Signed fractions of a second at nanosecond resolution of the span of time. Durations less than one second are represented with a 0 `seconds` field and a positive or negative `nanos` field. For durations of one second or more, a non-zero value for the `nanos` field must be of the same sign as the `seconds` field. Must be from -999,999,999 to +999,999,999 inclusive.
- `seconds` (Number) Signed seconds of the span of time. Must be from -315,576,000,000 to +315,576,000,000 inclusive. Note: these bounds are computed from: 60 sec/min * 60 min/hr * 24 hr/day * 365.25 days/year * 10000 years
